title: Routing, Via, Copy-to, and Distribution Intelligence

rules.source: SECNAV M-5216.5 Chapter 7 / Chapter 8 addressing conventions
rules.source_location: Requires refinement — Via numbering is described in figure examples and chapter narrative but not assigned a single paragraph number
rules.rule_text_summary: When two or more Via addressees are used, they must be numbered in routing order beginning with (1).

rules.source: SECNAV M-5216.5 Chapter 7 / Chapter 8 addressing conventions
rules.rule_text_summary: Via numbering must start at (1) and be consecutive.

rules.source: SECNAV M-5216.5 Chapter 7 / Chapter 8 addressing conventions
rules.rule_text_summary: A single Via addressee should not be numbered.

rules.source_location: Requires refinement — routing through channels / chain of command narrative
rules.rule_text_summary: Via text should name specific addressees rather than generic routing phrases such as 'through appropriate channels'.

rules.source: SECNAV M-5216.5 Chapter 2 and Chapter 7 copy distribution rules
rules.source_location: Chapter 2 copy distribution and need-to-know limitations; Chapter 7 addressing
rules.rule_text_summary: Copy-to lists should be limited to addressees with a need to know; excessive copy-to lists should be reviewed.

rules.source: SECNAV M-5216.5 Chapter 2 copy distribution rules
rules.rule_text_summary: Copy-to entries should be specific; vague or overly broad entries such as 'All Hands' or 'Distribution' should be reviewed.
The same addressee should not appear as both an action addressee (To or Via) and a Copy-to recipient.
Four or fewer action addressees may use To-line format; using Distribution-only with four or fewer entries is unusual unless copy variation or other reason exists.
To-plus-Distribution format should use a group title in the To line and individual members in the Distribution line.
If the office code is composed of only numbers, add the word "Code" before the numbers. Do not add the word "Code" before an office code that starts with a letter (e.g., "N" or "SUP").
rules.source_quote: If the office code is composed of only numbers, add the word "Code" before the numbers. Do not add the word "Code" before an office code that starts with a letter (e.g., "N" or "SUP").

rules.source_location: Chapter 7, Section 6, "From:" Line, subparagraph a. General
rules.rule_text_summary: Every standard letter must have a "From:" line, except a letter that will be used with a window envelope.
rules.source_quote: Every standard letter must have a "From:" line, except a letter that will be used with a window envelope.
